summary: Replayed ADR 0101 from the latest `origin/main` in an isolated worktree, fixed the remaining live automation defects surfaced by the first replay, verified both `make converge-openbao` and `make converge-step-ca` end to end, and confirmed the private OpenBao, Vaultwarden, and step-ca proxy paths through the host Tailscale address.

verification.observed: `tests/test_tls_cert_probe.py`, `tests/test_generate_cert_renewal_config.py`, `tests/test_tls_cert_drift.py`, `tests/test_platform_observation_tool.py`, and `tests/test_compose_runtime_secret_injection.py` all passed after adding worktree-aware trust-root resolution plus hour-based probe windows for short-lived certificates.

verification.check: Repository data-model and playbook syntax validation
verification.observed: `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate`, `python3 scripts/generate_cert_renewal_config.py --pretty`, `make syntax-check-openbao`, and `make syntax-check-step-ca` all completed successfully from the isolated live-apply worktree.

verification.check: OpenBao live replay and automation recovery
verification.observed: The first `make converge-openbao` replay from current `origin/main` exposed stale production inventory for `postgres-replica-lv3` and an OpenBao Docker publish failure after firewall reload. The branch now removes `postgres-replica-lv3` from active production targeting and adds Docker NAT/forward-chain recovery to the OpenBao role; a fresh `make converge-openbao` completed successfully against `docker-runtime-lv3` and `postgres-lv3`, including the dynamic PostgreSQL credential verification path.

verification.check: step-ca live replay and SSH trust rollout
verification.observed: `make converge-step-ca` completed successfully after aligning the live `step-ca` server-name set with the current controller topology, exporting `STEPPATH=/opt/step-ca/home` in the container, correcting the `step_ca` readiness probe command, adding short retries to early issuance verification, and removing the misplaced guest-firewall role from the Proxmox-host SSH trust play.
`lv3-openbao-cert-renew.timer` and `lv3-vaultwarden-cert-renew.timer` both report `Loaded: loaded` and `Active: active (waiting)` on `docker-runtime-lv3`.

`lv3-tailscale-proxy-openbao.socket` and `lv3-tailscale-proxy-step-ca.socket` are active on `proxmox-host`, and their paired proxy services are running against `10.10.10.20:8200` and `10.10.10.20:9000`.

verification.check: OpenBao certificate probe and mTLS listener behavior
verification.observed: `python3 scripts/tls_cert_probe.py --certificate-id openbao-proxy --fail-on never` reported `status: ok`, issuer `LV3 Internal CA Intermediate CA`, and `hours_remaining: 11`; `curl -vk https://100.64.0.1:8200/v1/sys/health` confirmed the host proxy presents the 24-hour `CN=100.64.0.1` certificate and requests a client certificate on connect.

`curl -vk --resolve vault.example.com:443:100.64.0.1 https://vault.example.com/` returned HTTP `200` with a 24-hour `CN=vault.example.com` certificate from the LV3 internal CA, confirming the service is healthy through the host Tailscale proxy.

verification.check: step-ca proxy health and live certificate verification
verification.observed: `curl --cacert /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/step-ca/certs/root_ca.crt https://100.64.0.1:9443/health` returned `{"status":"ok"}`, and `python3 scripts/tls_cert_probe.py --certificate-id step-ca-proxy --fail-on never` no longer reports a SAN mismatch against `100.64.0.1`.

notes: The branch intentionally does not modify protected integration files: `VERSION`, release sections in `changelog.md`, the top-level `README.md` integrated status summary, or `versions/stack.yaml`.

The shared certificate probe contract now uses the host Tailscale IP for `vaultwarden-private` while keeping `vault.example.com` as SNI, which removes a controller-local private-DNS dependency from the validation path.

The remaining merge-to-main work is limited to protected integration files and release bookkeeping on `main`; the live issues originally surfaced by this workstream (`postgres-replica-lv3` production targeting and the legacy `step-ca` proxy SAN) are resolved on this branch.
